name: Potato Cocktail Knishes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl with a dough hook, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. Add 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, beaten eggs, and 2 tablespoons water to the dry ingredients. Mix until a smooth dough forms (or mix by hand with a wooden spoon on a floured surface for about 6 minutes).
3. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise for 1 hour.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. In a medium skillet, heat oil or rendered chicken fat over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until just tender.
6. Mix sautéed onions into mashed potatoes; season with salt and pepper.
7. Divide dough into three equal pieces. Roll one piece into a thin rectangle about 10 inches long.
8. Spread about 2/3 cup of potato filling along the long side, near the edge, leaving a small border at each end.
9. Roll up the dough tightly into a log (jelly roll style) and pinch edges closed.
10. Repeat with remaining dough pieces and filling.
11. Arrange rolls on a baking sheet and bake for 40 minutes, or until golden brown.
12. Slice and serve. Unbaked knishes can be frozen; baked ones are good reheated later.
